items.title: Chronomancer Low Intensity Power 31k/33.5k
items.build:
3 Button: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYIMF2IO0KdNbA-DyQYhohe7bHCKwzICJQFKAZen4IhxG4tExyGA-e

5 Button: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYMMF2IO0KdNbA-DyQYhohe7bHCKwzICJQFKAZen4IhxG4tExyGA-e

Boon recommendation (look at gear): https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYMMF2IO0KdNTA-DSZYBRDsFuA4g8PjIkAVoAU8dijEGbg3SELbAA-e 

For the most part for boons it doesn't matter as long as you have: 50-60% boon duration + crit capped (100% crit chance) if it's easier for you to do it yourself.
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the three-button rundown, Phantasmal Swordsman is above Well of Action in priority. Spam Split Second, your F1, off cooldown. Make sure to finish your autoattack chains, because sword autoattacks are very strong and are the reason this build runs sword main hand. It is not a big deal if you miss one, but try to finish them. More or less, use sword skill five, Phantasmal Swordsman, over Well of Action, and use F1 off cooldown.

For the five-button version, it is mostly the same as the three-button version, except you add the heal skill and Well of Calamity. Your priority is sword skill five, optionally Signet of the Ether into sword skill five again, then both wells. Signet of the Ether resets your phantasm skills, so it resets sword five and lets you use Phantasmal Swordsman again. The wells have the same cooldown, so think of them as one big well action. Spam F1 off cooldown. As with the three-button build, try not to cancel your autoattack chains. If a cooldown is up, you can hold it briefly to let the third autoattack finish. The third hit, Stab, is the big one to watch for.

For the overall build rundown, skill two is Blurred Frenzy. You evade attacks and become temporarily invulnerable. If you want a sixth button, this is probably the best one to press. Illusionary Leap spawns a clone at the target, and Swap lets you swap with that clone, so you can think of it as a blink. Skill four spawns a clone if you are attacked and can be useful if you are about to be hit. It is also a CC skill. Press four twice to fire Counter Blade and get a two-second daze. Skill five is your big damage skill and spawns a phantasm.

The build takes Chronophantasma. Your phantasm attacks twice before becoming a clone, so you get two uses out of Phantasmal Swordsman. That is what the build relies on: squeezing more value out of fewer buttons with sword five. Trait-wise, there is not much complicated going on. The traits mostly buff damage, illusions, and phantasms. Danger Time is the reason Well of Action is good, because when you inflict slow, your and your illusions' critical strike damage is increased.

For F skills, F2 is not that useful here. F3 is a CC skill, so use it for CC if you have clones up. You daze for each clone you have, so more clones means more CC. Distortion makes you invulnerable for a little bit and can save you if you are about to die. Continuum Split is not used in this low-intensity build because it adds a lot of extra intensity, has movement displacement, and has a long cooldown, but it is useful. Anything you cast during Continuum Split is effectively reset when you shift back, so you can use it for extra CC or a stronger opener. For example, you can use Continuum Split with Signet of Humility for CC, then shift back and have the CC skill available again. You can also use it with Phantasmal Swordsman in an opener. Be careful because Continuum Split returns you to your starting spot, so using it in a bad position can get you killed.

For utilities, you probably do not want to change the heal skill. The elite can be Gravity Well or Signet of Humility. Gravity Well is strong CC over three seconds, while Signet of Humility applies a large amount of defiance break immediately. Mass Invisibility is extremely niche. Time Warp can be useful if your group is struggling with quickness or if you play this as a quickness build, but I usually take Signet of Humility or Gravity Well.

Mesmer has a lot of useful utility. If you are playing the well build, leave the wells as they are. If you are playing the three-button build, you should have a condition signet slot you can swap. Well of Precognition gives stability and aegis to you and your group. Well of Senility is CC. Signet of Domination also stuns when activated and can be useful if you want a big stun. Feedback is a projectile reflect and is useful when you need reflects, such as Matthias. Null Field and Phantasmal Disenchanter are boon strips. Portal Entre and Portal Exeunt are the famous mesmer portal and have many useful cases. Blink is also very useful for repositioning, such as on Sabetha when dodging the flame wall.

To play this build as quickness or alacrity, look at the last Chronomancer trait line. Stretched Time makes you alacrity DPS, and Seize the Moment makes you quickness DPS. By default, the build uses Chronophantasma. Taking one of those boon traits makes it so phantasms or shatters grant alacrity or quickness. With the way this build works, camping sword/sword and spamming your buttons does not create enough clones or phantasms by itself, so you need a lot of boon duration. I recommend at least 50%, and probably 60% to 70% if you want to be safe and have comfortable overflow.

The showcased setup uses Diviner's accessories for power, precision, ferocity, and concentration. Concentration is boon duration. Since Diviner's trades away some precision, the setup uses Assassin's armor to make up for the lost precision and stay crit capped, with Berserker swords using Force and Accuracy. To maintain quickness or alacrity, you need a solid amount of boon duration.

You probably cannot play the three-button version as a boon giver without changes, because it does not use Signet of the Ether. If you want to play three-button boons, drop Well of Action and use Signet of the Ether so you can get more Phantasmal Swordsmen out. Also try to wait on your first F1 until you have at least two clones, so the shatter grants more quickness or alacrity. You can also add Illusionary Leap, skill three, to spawn more clones for shatters, but that is another button.

If you are specifically playing quickness with Seize the Moment, Time Warp is also an option. You lose out on CC, but it can strengthen quickness uptime and may let you run a little less boon duration. Overall, add enough boon duration, use Signet of the Ether for more phantasms, and use your shatters with clones to maintain boons.

items.title: 3 Button Chronomancer Condi 32.3k
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, basically all you want to do is spam your Phantasmal Warlock, use your heal skill to reset your Phantasmal Warlock, and spam Rewinder, which is your F2, whenever you have three clones. If you're looking at the four-button build, over the adds it's just spamming Chaos Armor. If you're looking at the three-button build, I would recommend looking at the four-button build because it's basically zero effort for a pretty substantial DPS increase.

For your notable skills, Staff 5 is a CC, F3 is a CC, and F4 is Distortion, an invulnerability. These builds don't use Continuum Split, but you can use Continuum Split to combo with an important utility skill, like a really big CC skill, and do a lot of breakbar damage. One of the more popular combos is Continuum Splitting a Moa Signet, which is your Signet of Humility.

For utilities, your Signet of Domination is a CC. I would recommend not using it unless you absolutely have to because it is a substantial DPS loss. You can run Gravity Well, which is a slightly less bursty CC option than Moa Signet. There are a few really good utilities that Mesmer has. You can replace your Signet of Illusions with one of them. Well of Precognition is stability and Aegis spam. Well of Senility is CC and boon strip. Feedback is a reflection dome. Portal Entre is the classic Mesmer portal. Phantasmal Disenchanter is also really good if you need boon strip. Blink is useful for dodging mechanics like Sabetha's flame wall.

That's the rundown of condi Chrono. I hope you all enjoy the build, and thank you.
